factors.summary: The company is facing potential material litigation risks.

evidence.text: In August 2018, a putative shareholder class action lawsuit was filed... The actions were consolidated... and the Public Employees’ Retirement System of Mississippi was appointed lead plaintiff... Nielsen intends to defend these lawsuits vigorously.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces potential material litigation risks, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable as more than 70% of annual revenue is committed under contracts.

evidence.text: Typically, before the start of each year, more than 70% of our annual revenue has been committed under contracts in our combined Nielsen Global Connect and Nielsen Global Media segments, which provides us with a greater degree of stability for our revenue and allows us to more effectively manage our profitability and cash flows.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A stable order backlog with over 70% of annual revenue committed suggests strong demand and revenue stability.

factors.summary: Management is cautious with ongoing focus on cost management and restructuring.

evidence.text: We have taken measures to protect the health and safety of our employees, their families and our clients, with a large majority of our worldwide workforce working from home. We have halted in-store field research and in-person client engagements in many markets and are adapting processes and developing innovative solutions to ensure continuity of critical business processes.

evidence.text: We continue to believe that a separation of Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect is the best path forward. We continue to make operational progress towards separation, and expect the closing to take place in the first quarter of 2021.

There was no specific mention of buybacks or dividends in the provided text.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a notable increase from $183 million to $245 million over the past year. Despite this positive trend, the company maintains a stable cash generation and sufficient liquidity to meet its obligations.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $245 million for the six months ended June 30, 2020, as compared to $183 million for the six months ended June 30, 2019. This increase... was primarily due to working capital timing and lower income tax payments and interest payments.
We have sufficient liquidity to satisfy our cash needs and will take additional actions as required.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving with a notable increase.

factors.summary: The company is actively managing its debt leverage by refinancing at lower interest rates, focusing on maturities, liquidity, and operating cash flows to reduce financial risk.

evidence.text: In June 2020, we entered into a Credit Agreement that provides for: (i) a new dollar term loan facility... and (ii) a new euro term loan facility... The proceeds were used to redeem all of the $800 million outstanding aggregate principal amount of their 4.500% Notes due 2020 and redeem $200 million of the $625 million outstanding aggregate principal amount of its 5.5.00% Senior Notes due 2021.
Our high level of indebtedness requires active management of our debt profile, with a focus on underlying maturities, interest rate risk, liquidity and operating cash flows.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively managing debt by refinancing at lower rates, reducing financial risk.

factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating, particularly in end markets affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, with notable impacts on sports, retail measurement services, and custom insights.

evidence.text: Revenues decreased 8.1% to $1,496 million for the three months ended June 30, 2020 from $1,628 million for the three months ended June 30, 2019, or a decrease of 5.9% on a constant currency basis.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: The decrease in operating income within our Connect segment was primarily driven by the revenue decrease due to the COVID-19 pandemic discussed above, an increase in restructuring charges and depreciation and amortization expense, as well as our continued global investments in our services, including retailer investments for the three months ended June 30, 2020.

We faced increased pressure in both Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect in the second quarter. For Nielsen Global Media, this pressure was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on sports and non-contracted revenue. For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and innovation.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to COVID-19 impacts in key markets.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are declining, particularly in segments affected by the COVID-19 pandemic. This decline is attributed to the halt of in-store field research and in-person client engagements in many markets, which have been impacted by the pandemic.

evidence.text: Revenues from Predict/Activate decreased 15.9% to $190 million, or a decrease of 13.2% on a constant currency basis, reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly in custom insights and innovation, partially offset by the January 2020 acquisition of Precima.

We have halted in-store field research and in-person client engagements in many markets and are adapting processes and developing innovative solutions to ensure continuity of critical business processes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes, especially due to pandemic-related restrictions.

factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is decelerating across both its Connect and Media segments, with the deceleration being more pronounced in the Media segment compared to the Connect segment. The deceleration is attributed to the impact of COVID-19, particularly affecting sports and non-contracted revenue in Media and retail measurement services in Connect.

evidence.text: Revenues within our Connect segment decreased 11.3%, or a decrease of 7.4% on a constant currency basis. Revenues within our Media segment decreased 5.3%, or a decrease of 4.6% on a constant currency basis.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Revenues within our Connect segment decreased 7.1%, or a decrease of 3.6% on a constant currency basis. Revenues within our Media segment decreased 1.7%, or a decrease of 1.1% on a constant currency basis.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We faced increased pressure in both Nielsen Global Media and Nielsen Global Connect in the second quarter. For Nielsen Global Media, this pressure was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on sports and non-contracted revenue. For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and innovation.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is decelerating across segments due to COVID-19.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure and compressing, primarily due to restructuring charges, impairment of assets, and increased depreciation and amortization expenses. The company anticipates significant pre-tax annual run-rate savings from restructuring actions and cost-savings initiatives, but these short-term expenses are negatively impacting current margins.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are compressing, driven by restructuring charges, impairment of assets, and other non-operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Operating income for the three months ended June 30, 2020 was $28 million as compared to $249 million for the three months ended June 30, 2019. The decrease was primarily due to restructuring charges, impairment of other long-lived assets, and other non-operating items.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Operating income for the six months ended June 30, 2020 was $126 million as compared to $423 million for the six months ended June 30, 2019. This decrease was primarily driven by higher restructuring charges, depreciation and amortization expense, and impairment of other long-lived assets.

We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings. We expect 2020 pre-tax restructuring charges of $150 to $170 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure from restructuring and other expenses.

factors.summary: The company's cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, though some initiatives are behind schedule. The restructuring plan is expected to be substantially completed in 2020, with annual pre-tax savings of approximately $250 million. However, cash payments for severance costs will continue into late 2021, indicating a mixed progress in achieving the timeline.

evidence.text: We recorded $84 million in restructuring charges for the three months ended June 30, 2020. These charges primarily related to severance costs associated with employee severance packages. We expect the plan to be substantially completed in 2020. Cash payments for the severance costs will continue into late 2021.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We recorded $95 million in restructuring charges for the six months ended June 30, 2020. These charges primarily related to severance costs associated with employee separation packages. The plan is expected to be substantially completed in 2020, with cash payments continuing into late 2021.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We expect the Restructuring Plan to be substantially completed in 2020 and for restructuring actions and other permanent cost-savings initiatives to drive approximately $250 million in pre-tax annual run-rate savings. We expect 2020 pre-tax restructuring charges of $150 to $170 million.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are ongoing but with mixed progress on timelines.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are positively impacting cost savings and operational efficiency across multiple segments, though some areas like the Media segment experienced cost increases due to investments.

evidence.text: The decrease in cost of revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2020 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2019 was primarily due to our productivity initiatives and temporary cost savings due to actions taken in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, partially offset by global investments in our services, including retailer investments.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Costs within our Connect segment decreased 3.6%, or a decrease of 0.8% on a constant currency basis, primarily due to our productivity initiatives and lower variable costs associated with lower revenues. Costs within our Media segment increased 3.9%, or an increase of 4.7% on a constant currency basis, primarily due to the impact of our investments and higher spending on product portfolio management initiatives, partially offset by productivity initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity initiatives are yielding cost savings and efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: The forward guidance is mixed, with management not explicitly changing their outlook but expressing caution due to ongoing economic uncertainty and potential impacts from COVID-19.

evidence.text: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance in the provided text.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We believe we have a sound plan in place to mitigate the financial impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic in the face of ongoing economic uncertainty. We have taken aggressive cost actions to date and continue to closely monitor the situation.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious but has not changed guidance.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a significant headwind due to the global impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, which has negatively affected various end markets, disrupted supply chains, and reduced global demand. The pandemic has led to shelter-in-place orders, travel restrictions, and significant disruptions in financial markets, all of which have contributed to a challenging economic environment. Revenues have decreased, and this economic downturn has impacted business segments, leading to lower operating income.

evidence.text: Revenues decreased 8.1% to $1,496 million for the three months ended June 30, 2020 from $1,628 million for the three months ended June 30, 2019, or a decrease of 5.9% on a constant currency basis. The decrease was primarily due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on various end markets.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: The global outbreak of the novel coronavirus ('COVID-19') was categorized as a pandemic by the World Health Organization and has negatively affected the global economy, disrupted global supply chains, resulted in significant travel and transport restrictions, including mandated closures and orders to 'shelter-in-place,' and created significant disruption of the financial markets.

The decrease in operating income within our Connect segment was primarily driven by the revenue decrease due to the COVID-19 pandemic discussed above, an increase in restructuring charges and depreciation and amortization expense, as well as our continued global investments in our services, including retailer investments for the three months ended June 30, 2020.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily related to the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on demand and revenue, as well as separation-related costs. While cost management measures have been implemented, there is potential economic uncertainty that could further affect financial performance.

evidence.text: The increase in operating expenses was primarily driven by separation-related costs, partially offset by lower business optimization costs and transaction related costs for the three months ended June 30, 2020. The COVID-19 pandemic continues to impact demand in various end markets, leading to revenue decreases and higher restructuring charges.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We believe we have a sound plan in place to mitigate the financial impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic in the face of ongoing economic uncertainty. We have taken aggressive cost actions to date and continue to closely monitor the situation.

factors.summary: The end markets are experiencing negative momentum, particularly in sports, traditional retail, local television, Telecom, and media segments, due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Revenues from Audience Measurement decreased 0.7%, or a decrease of 0.2% on a constant currency basis, reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports and non-contracted revenue and ongoing pressure in local television.

Plan/Optimize revenues decreased 4.4%, or a decrease of 3.5% on a constant currency basis, primarily reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sports, Gracenote auto and short-cycle revenue and ongoing pressure in Telecom.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

evidence.text: For Nielsen Global Media, this pressure was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on sports and non-contracted revenue.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

evidence.text: For Nielsen Global Connect, this was primarily due to the impact of COVID-19 on retail measurement services in markets that are heavy in traditional trade as well as pressures on custom insights and innovation.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies / Goodwill and Indefinite-Lived Intangible Assets

factors.summary: The company faces challenges from unfavorable foreign exchange movements, particularly involving the Euro against the U.S. Dollar, which created a headwind for operating results. However, management of FX exposure through constant currency reporting indicates stable performance when excluding exchange rate fluctuations.

evidence.text: Fluctuations in the value of foreign currencies relative to the U.S. Dollar, primarily the Euro, have a significant effect on our operating results. The average U.S. Dollar to Euro exchange rate was $1.10 to €1.00 for the six months ended June 30, 2020 as compared to $1.13 to €.00 for the six months ended June 30, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to the Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We evaluate our results of operations on both an as reported and a constant currency basis. The constant currency presentation is a non-GAAP financial measure, which excludes the impact of period-over-period fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates.
